ideas.title: Behavioral Security Training
ideas.summary: Security that changes behavior at the moment of risk, not a yearly video you forget.
ideas.intent: Sketch one micro-moment intervention that interrupts a risky click in real time.
ideas.lastInsight: Compliance training is passive; behavior change needs friction at the moment of risk.

connections.relation: Both act in the moment it matters — a risky click in one, a daily signal in the other — not after the fact.

ideas.title: Passive Life Analytics
ideas.summary: Gentle reflection from the data your devices already collect. No new tracking.
ideas.intent: List every signal my phone already records without any new hardware.
ideas.lastInsight: The data already exists — the missing piece is gentle synthesis, not more tracking.

connections.relation: Both believe change happens in the moment, and that quiet, well-timed cues beat dashboards.

ideas.title: Systems Thinking
ideas.summary: Every engineered system is a transformation of inputs into outputs under constraints — mastering that structure transfers across any domain.
ideas.intent: Develop domain-independent systems thinking that transfers across software, logistics, manufacturing, healthcare, and other industries. Start by studying one unfamiliar industry each week using the same framework.
ideas.lastInsight: Every engineered system can be understood as a transformation of inputs into outputs under constraints; mastering systems thinking means recognizing that structure regardless of domain.
